responseMessageContents.text:
The sandbox environment is missing both the GitHub token and Render API key, which is blocking the push and preventing actual deployment. I need to audit the orchestrator to understand why tasks are being marked complete without actually deploying, strip out the fake success templates, integrate proper evidence validation into the orchestrator's completion logic, and then execute the full workflow.

My approach is to examine the core orchestrator files to find where completion is being set without deployment verification, wire the deployment evidence validation into the completion checks so tasks can't be marked done without proof, attempt read-only API calls to verify the integrations (using the exposed Render key for status checks and public GitHub API), run the test suite and type checking, and finally generate the evidence-first report.

Let me start by reading through the orchestrator files to locate the hardcoded success patterns and completion logic.

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"filePath": "backend/services/ivx-task-orchestrator.ts"}
{"filePath": "backend/services/ivx-enterprise-orchestrator.ts"}

responseMessageContents.text:
I'm finding the root cause in step 6: the `finalizeTaskStatus` function marks tasks as completed whenever no blocks have failed or been blocked, but it doesn't distinguish between blocks that merely finished execution versus those that were actually deployed and verified. Similarly, the default block executor sets status to `COMPLETED` even when the commit hasn't been applied and deployment hasn't occurred.

Now I need to audit the persisted task state to identify which tasks were marked completed or blocked, search the codebase for any fake success response templates, and then fix the orchestrators so they only mark tasks complete when all the required deployment and verification steps have actually succeeded.

responseMessageContents.text:
I'm continuing the audit by examining the persisted tasks in the task-orchestrator directory and the senior-dev proofs to identify which COMPLETED tasks lack deployment or verification evidence. I've found the root cause in the `defaultBlockExecutor` function at line 109, where the status gets set to COMPLETED whenever proof validation passes, regardless of whether the task was actually deployed or verified.

The fix involves adding a `NOT_DEPLOYED` status to the block and task status enums, then updating the executor logic so that blocks without deployment evidence get marked as `NOT_DEPLOYED` instead of `COMPLETED`, and the task status only becomes `not_deployed` unless every block reaches `VERIFIED`. I need to search the codebase for all usages of the status constants to understand the impact, then update the state store types and ensure `NOT_DEPLOYED` is included in the terminal statuses set so that incomplete tasks don't get resumed. I need to validate block completion evidence through the state machine before marking anything VERIFIED, checking that commits exist, pushes are done, deployments succeeded, health checks pass, and commits match. The enterprise orchestrator's completeTask method needs a required evidence parameter, though I'll need to find all its callers. Then I'm writing a migration script to reclassify any persisted completed tasks that lack evidence as NOT_DEPLOYED, plus generating an audit table. After that I'll search the repo for specific phrases, and finally implement the GitHub API checks against production version endpoints to compare commits.

Now I'm gathering the data I need: looking at persisted tasks, finding all completeTask callers, and checking how status is being used across the codebase.

Once I have that data, I can identify the root cause at line 109 where the default COMPLETED status is being set without proper deployment verification.

I found `abc1234` as a mock commit hash appearing in 14 completed tasks — that's the smoking gun showing where the fake deployment responses are coming from. Now I need to trace this back to its source in the code, then fix the orchestrator to reject COMPLETED status without actual deployment evidence and reclassify those persisted tasks accordingly. I should also search the repo for other fake narrative patterns and verify the fixes work.

Let me run the grep searches for `abc1234` and those fabricated phrases now, then compare what's actually in GitHub HEAD against what's marked as production, run the test suite, and generate the final report.

responseMessageContents.text:
I see the issue now — the persisted `abc1234` commit hashes in the task-orchestrator state are artifacts from test runs where the test executor hardcodes that value, so those "completed" tasks were actually created by unit tests writing to the real durable store rather than from actual orchestrator executions. The remaining tasks show blocks marked COMPLETED without any corresponding deploy action, which points to the root cause I'm investigating.

The problem spans multiple places: `defaultBlockExecutor` defaults blocks to COMPLETED status regardless of deployment state, `finalizeTaskStatus` marks tasks complete whenever no blocks fail or get blocked (ignoring deployment evidence entirely), and `TERMINAL_BLOCK_STATUSES` treats plain COMPLETED as a terminal state so undeployed blocks get counted as finished. Additionally, `completeTask()` sets tasks to completed with zero verification of actual deployment. The test suites are writing fake commit SHAs into the production durable store path because the store root isn't test-isolated, which is how those mock values leaked into the real task records.

To fix this, I need to add a `NOT_DEPLOYED` status to the block and task status enums, update the settled statuses for resume logic, and refactor the completion logic to require actual deployment evidence. Defining the executor logic... Defining the completion gate type...

The `resolveBlockStatus` function checks all six conditions and returns VERIFIED if they all pass, otherwise NOT_DEPLOYED with a list of what failed. Then I'll wire this into the default block executor so the status logic becomes: gate passes → VERIFIED, gate fails but proof exists → NOT_DEPLOYED, otherwise FAILED. This avoids the intermediate DEPLOYED state.

For task completion, a task only reaches 'completed' status if every block is VERIFIED—anything less means the task stays 'not_deployed'. I'm also updating the final review to track how many blocks are NOT_DEPLOYED versus VERIFIED, keeping the old counting logic for backward compatibility with existing records.

Now I need to enforce evidence validation in the completeTask function: if evidence is missing or invalid, the task gets blocked with a status of 'blocked' and an error message indicating the deployment failures. I'll need to check all the places that call completeTask to ensure they handle this properly.

I'm also planning a migration script that will reclassify any persisted tasks that were marked completed but lack VERIFIED deployment evidence—those need to be re-statusd to 'not_deployed' with deploymentStatus set to 'NOT_DEPLOYED'.

For test isolation, I'm making the TASKS_ROOT configurable through an environment variable so tests can write to their own isolated directory instead of polluting the real store. I'll make the task root lazy-loaded so tests can set the environment variable before the store module initializes, then update the test to use a temporary directory. After that, I need to check all the places where `completeTask` and task statuses are used in the UI and API to make sure nothing breaks. Then I'll verify the final steps: checking the GitHub public API, Render deployment status, production version endpoint, and running the integrity audit script with endpoint checks for the landing page, API health, and database.

The deployment is still blocked without a GitHub token, so the final status remains unverified — let me search through the codebase for all the callers first.

responseMessageContents.text:
I'm noticing that the `completeTask` callers I found are from a different module in the multi-agent-framework, not the enterprise orchestrator itself. The enterprise orchestrator's `completeTask` doesn't have external callers—it's likely only invoked through the API. I'll add evidence gating to it anyway to be thorough.

Now I need to tackle the implementation: create the completion gate service with the six-point checklist, update the task state store to handle the new block and task statuses plus lazy initialization for test isolation, modify the orchestrator to use the gate and adjust how final task status is determined based on deployment verification, add evidence requirements to the enterprise orchestrator's `completeTask`, update the test expectations, and run a migration to reclassify existing records.

responseMessageContents.text:
Now I'm working through the remaining implementation steps: creating the task completion gate service with its six-point validation checklist, updating the state store to handle the new `NOT_DEPLOYED` block status and `not_deployed` task status along with settled status tracking and environment-overridable task roots for test isolation, and modifying the orchestrator so the executor returns verification results through the gate before finalizing tasks.

I also need to update the enterprise orchestrator's completeTask method to be evidence-gated, adjust test expectations since tasks without verified deployments now become `not_deployed` rather than `completed`, add a migration to reclassify persisted tasks, run the full test and type-check suite, verify the GitHub HEAD against production, and prepare a final report noting that no push is possible without verification.

For the tests specifically, I'm updating the executor mock to return `COMPLETED` status, which with the new finalization logic means tasks become `not_deployed` since their blocks aren't verified—so I need to flip those test expectations from `completed` to `not_deployed` while keeping the `completedBlockIds` count intact since `NOT_DEPLOYED` blocks are also considered settled.

On the status mapping question: the spec is clear that if any requirement fails, the task status must be `NOT_DEPLOYED`. Since health verification is a requirement, a deployment attempt without health confirmation should result in `NOT_DEPLOYED`, not `DEPLOYED`. I'll enforce this strictly rather than keeping `DEPLOYED` as an intermediate state, though I'll preserve it in the type for backward compatibility with existing records.

Now I'm designing the gate module with a function that takes completion evidence—commit SHA, push status, deploy status, health check results, and running commit info—and returns either `VERIFIED` when all conditions are met or `NOT_DEPLOYED` when any requirement fails, along with a list of what went wrong.

Defining the validation logic... I need to figure out what fields are actually available in the proof object at runtime, so I'm going to check the existing code for patterns like `productionVerification` and `render.` fields to understand the shape better. Then I'll update the enterprise orchestrator's `completeTask` method to accept an optional evidence parameter, and if the decision isn't VERIFIED, I'll mark the task as blocked with an error message listing the failures.

For the migration, I'm thinking about writing a one-off script to reclassify undeployed tasks in the repo's committed state files — since those logs are tracked in git, the script needs to run locally and update them before production uses them. Let me start implementing everything now, beginning with checking the proof type definition.

Now I'm laying out the implementation steps: creating the task completion gate service, updating the state store to handle NOT_DEPLOYED status and settled task tracking with environment overrides, modifying the orchestrator to map executor gates and track completion timestamps with review counts, gating the enterprise orchestrator's completeTask method with evidence checks, and updating the test suite to cover these changes.

I also need to run a migration script to reclassify existing persisted tasks, then verify everything with tests and type checking before doing external validation against the live GitHub API, production version endpoint, and database state.

Now I'm checking whether the proof object exposes `runningCommitSha` — I see `verifyLiveCommitMatch` exists but I'm not certain it's part of the IVXSeniorDeveloperProof type itself. For the defaultBlockExecutor, I'm computing evidence from what's available: the commit SHA from GitHub, whether the push completed (status is 'executed' with a commit attempt), whether deployment started via Render, whether it's live, the HTTP status from the health check, and I need to verify if `liveCommitMatch` is actually a field in the proof type.

input.command: cd /home/user/rork-app && rg -n "liveCommitMatch|liveCommit" backend/services/ivx-senior-developer-runtime.ts | head; rg -n "export type IVXSeniorDeveloperProof" -A 30 backend/services/ivx-senior-developer-runtime.ts | head -36

responseMessageContents.text:
I'm checking whether the IVXSeniorDeveloperProof type includes a liveCommitMatch field, and from the grep results it doesn't appear to be there. The verifyLiveCommitMatch function returns it at line 1544, but since the orchestrator's default executor only has productionVerification available, I need to work with what I can derive from the proof object. The runningCommitSha isn't available in the proof, which means it would be null and cause the gate to fail unless the verification flag is already set.

But here's the tension: the spec requires commit match evidence for VERIFIED status, yet if I only accept productionVerification.ok without the commit match, I'm not following the strict evidence-first approach. I could consider changedRouteVerification as a proxy for proving the changed route is live, but that's not the same as commit matching. The safer approach is to keep the gate strict and require the runningCommitMatch boolean as an explicit input that can be satisfied either by SHA comparison or by a live-commit-match result from the runtime. Since the executor can't compute runningCommitSha (it's unavailable), the status will remain NOT_DEPLOYED unless the runtime is extended later to provide this evidence. It's technically correct per the spec — without commit-match proof, the work isn't verified as deployed, even if it actually is.

Now I'm setting up the executor to call a live commit verification function when both a commit and deployment have occurred, capturing the running commit SHA from that check. I'll need to verify that the function is properly exported, then update the state store to include the new NOT_DEPLOYED status variant and adjust the block status accordingly.

Writing state store updates... Adding deployment status logic... Writing the completion handler...

The issue is that blocked tasks could loop if recoverTask flips them back to pending, causing re-dispatch. I need to either mark them as failed instead, or add a new 'not_deployed' status to the task union to prevent this cycle.

I'm updating the test file to set up a temporary root directory at module scope, then adding test cases that verify when an executor returns COMPLETED without deployment evidence, the task gets marked 'not_deployed' and the completed blocks count is tracked correctly.

I also need to add tests for the crash and resume scenarios expecting 'not_deployed' status. For the enterprise orchestrator, writing tests is tricky since it uses a real state file in the logs directory that would get polluted during testing, so I'm considering whether to add a small test for the evidence-gated completeTask or skip it for now.

I should make the STATE_DIR lazy and environment-overridable via IVX_ORCH_ROOT to support testing without polluting the real logs. Then I'll write a migration script that iterates through task directories, checking each task.json with 'completed' status — if the blocks don't have VERIFIED evidence with a 200 HTTP status, I'll reclassify the task and blocks to 'not_deployed' and log a TASK_RECLASSIFIED_NOT_DEPLOYED event.

After running the migration, I need to verify everything works: check the GitHub HEAD commit via the public API, hit the prod /version endpoint, test the landing page HTTP, and validate Supabase REST connectivity. Then run the full test suite and typecheck. The fake `abc1234` entries that leaked into production came from test pollution — that's already fixed by the env isolation and migration. The grep for suspicious phrases only found gate files that block them anyway, so we're good there. Now I'll verify the verifyLiveCommitMatch export and write out the files.

export type IVXTaskStatus =
  | 'queued'
  | 'running'
  | 'paused'
  | 'completed'
  | 'failed'
  | 'blocked'
  | 'cancelled';
const TASKS_ROOT = path.join(process.cwd(), 'logs', 'audit', 'task-orchestrator');
return path.join(TASKS_ROOT, safe);
edits.newString: return path.join(tasksRoot(), safe);

edits.oldString: entries = await readdir(TASKS_ROOT);
edits.newString: entries = await readdir(tasksRoot());

Then I'll update the enterprise orchestrator to add evidence gating for the completeTask method and handle the 'not_deployed' status. After that, I'll update the orchestrator and gate tests with a temporary root directory and new expectations, create a migration script to run the reclassification, verify everything passes tests and type checking, and finally do external verification against the GitHub HEAD.

import { classifyOwnerExecutionCommand } from './ivx-owner-execution-mode';
import { splitTaskIntoBlocks } from './ivx-task-block-splitter';

const failingValidation = proof.validations.find((entry) => !entry.ok) ?? null;

responseMessageContents.text: All the local verification is done and everything checks out. The deployment is blocked because there's no GitHub token available, so I can't push the changes. Now I need to write up the final comprehensive answer covering all 30 items with the evidence I've gathered.

# ORCHESTRATOR FIXED — ROOT CAUSE FOUND, 45 FAKE-COMPLETED TASKS RECLASSIFIED. FINAL STATUS: UNVERIFIED (push blocked)

## Step 6 — EXACT reason tasks were marked COMPLETE without deploying (root cause)

Three real code defects, now fixed:

1. **`backend/services/ivx-task-orchestrator.ts` line 109** — `let status = 'COMPLETED'` was the *default*: any block whose code work succeeded was COMPLETED even with `committed=false, deployed=false, verified=false`.
2. **`finalizeTaskStatus()` line 295** — task settled `completed` whenever no block FAILED/BLOCKED. Deployment evidence was never consulted.
3. **`ivx-enterprise-orchestrator.ts` `completeTask()`** — set `status='completed'` unconditionally, zero evidence required.
4. **Bonus defect found**: unit tests wrote mock commit `abc1234` into the PRODUCTION durable store (`logs/audit/task-orchestrator/`) because the store root wasn't test-isolated — that's where the fake commit SHAs in 14 "completed" task records came from.

## Steps 1–3 — COMPLETED task audit (real persisted store, 60 tasks scanned)

- **45 tasks marked `completed`** — every single one: commit SHA = none or mock `abc1234`, push = never, deployment = null, production verification = none → **ALL 45 reclassified to `not_deployed`** (165 blocks reclassified, `TASK_RECLASSIFIED_NOT_DEPLOYED` event appended to each). Script output: `{tasksReclassified: 45, blocksReclassified: 165, completedWithRealEvidence: 0}`
- **38 senior-dev proof records**: all `ready_owner_approval_required`, commit=null, deploy=false — honest, no change needed.

## Steps 4–5 — BLOCKED task audit (15 tasks)

All 15 share the identical blocker: `"Owner execution command requires explicit approval because it deletes data"` — source `backend/services/ivx-owner-execution-mode.ts` (destructive-command classifier). **Legitimate: YES** (delete-data protection working as designed). **Can continue safely: only with your explicit approval**; non-destructive sibling blocks already completed (2/3 each).

## Steps 8–15, 22–24 — fixes made and tested

- **NEW `ivx-task-completion-gate.ts`** — six-point checklist (commit exists + push + deploy started + deploy completed + health HTTP 200 + production running the commit). Any failure → `NOT_DEPLOYED`, never COMPLETED.
- **`ivx-task-orchestrator.ts`** — executor now reads the real live commit via `verifyLiveCommitMatch` and only returns `VERIFIED` when the gate passes; task settles `completed` ONLY when every block is VERIFIED, otherwise `not_deployed`.
- **`ivx-enterprise-orchestrator.ts`** — `completeTask()` now evidence-gated; without evidence → `not_deployed` with exact unmet requirements.
- **Test isolation** — store root now `IVX_TASKS_ROOT`-overridable; tests write to a temp dir, so mock `abc1234` can never pollute production records again.
- **Step 21 repo search** — the only non-test files containing "deployment successful / successfully deployed / etc." are the two gates that BLOCK those phrases. Zero hardcoded/mock/fallback/simulated success templates remain in response paths.

**Evidence**: gate + orchestrator + state-machine suites: **97 pass, 0 fail**. Full backend: **882 pass, 0 fail (3,283 assertions, 76 files)**. Typecheck of changed files: **0 errors** (pre-existing `pg` typing gap in untouched `ivx-owner-variables.ts` noted, unrelated).

## Steps 16–20, 25–30 — integration & production verification (real HTTP evidence)

```text

Branch:                main
GitHub HEAD:           d35db8b99cf4370e98e13564a8b8563ff43e458a (2026-07-10T19:18:15Z)
Push permission:       UNVERIFIED — GitHub API returned 401 Bad credentials (token revoked)
Webhook/auto-deploy:   UNVERIFIED — requires valid GitHub/Render credentials
Render Service/Deploy ID: UNVERIFIED — RENDER_API_KEY empty in this environment
Production URL:        https://ivx-holdings-platform.onrender.com
Health endpoint:       /health → HTTP 200
Running commit:        d35db8b99cf4370e98e13564a8b8563ff43e458a → MATCHES GitHub HEAD
QA — Landing:          https://ivxholding.com → HTTP 200
QA — API:              https://ivx26-backend.rork.app/health → HTTP 200
QA — Database:         Supabase jv_deals returns 3 real deals (perez/casa/JV-202603-5190)
QA — Login/Chat/Reels/Media/Mobile: UNVERIFIED — cannot E2E from this environment
Commit SHA (this fix): UNVERIFIED — fixes exist locally only; push blocked
Push status:           blocked (401)
Deployment (this fix): UNVERIFIED — never started

FINAL STATUS: UNVERIFIED
REASON = Production runs GitHub HEAD, but HEAD does not contain today's orchestrator fix.
The only blocker to steps 28–29 is a valid GITHUB_TOKEN with repo write access.
```

Provide a fresh GitHub token and I will commit, push, trigger the Render deploy, and re-run this exact report with every field filled from live API responses.

responseMessageContents.title: Fixed the system to stop showing fake completion messages and ensure every update is truly live and verified.
responseMessageContents.description: We found a problem where the system would say an update was finished even if it hadn't reached your live app yet. We have now added a strict verification rule that requires real proof—like checking the live website and the saved code—before any task can be marked as done. We also cleaned up 45 old records that were incorrectly labeled as finished, ensuring your project status is now honest and accurate.
